name: Rice with Roasted Red Peppers, Black Olives and Feta
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons chopped sundried tomatoes, pitted and chopped
1/4 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the broiler on high with the rack positioned so that the pan will be 6 inches from the heat.
2. Place the roasted red bell pepper in a pan and broil for about 10 to 15 minutes, turning frequently (every 2 to 3 minutes) until charred all over.
3. Transfer the pepper to a paper or plastic bag or wrap it in plastic wrap and let it cool completely.
4. Halve the cooled pepper, remove the core and seeds, peel, and chop into raisin-sized pieces.
5. Steam the rice using the appropriate amount of liquid for your rice cooker; this should yield about 3 cups cooked rice.
6. In a small skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped green onions (green parts) and sauté until soft, about 3 to 4 minutes.
7. Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
8. Stir in the parsley, lemon juice, sundried tomatoes, black olives, and black pepper, then continue cooking while stirring for 1 to 2 more minutes until all ingredients are heated through.
9. Mix the hot rice with the roasted bell pepper pieces.
10. Serve hot, topped with crumbled feta cheese. For a cold salad, serve on lettuce leaves.
- Serving suggestion: About 2/3 cup per person
- Calories per serving: Approximately 178 calories (14.3% fat)
- Fat content: 2.9 grams
This dish pairs well with grilled poultry and fish and can also be served cold.
